8	
9	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
10	
11	- **A "missing base change" between affine section rings is usually NOT missing — build it from `Algebra.IsEpi`
12	  (iter-056, GF; dissolved a 5-iter STUCK).** The open-immersion flat-epimorphism base change
13	  `IsBaseChange Γ(S,V) (id)` framed as "Mathlib-absent" was fully constructible. Two reusable bricks:
14	  (1) `gf_isEpi_restrict_of_affine_le`: for affine `U ≤ V`, `Γ(S,V)→Γ(S,U)` is `Algebra.IsEpi`. Route:
15	  `rw [← CommRingCat.epi_iff_epi]`; `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` gives `Spec.map ρ ≫ hV.fromSpec = hU.fromSpec`
16	  with `hU.fromSpec` an open-immersion **mono** ⟹ `Mono (Spec.map ρ)` (`mono_of_mono`); reflect through the
17	  fully-faithful `Scheme.Spec` (`Spec.fullyFaithful.faithful` → `Functor.mono_of_mono_map`) to `Mono ρ.op`, then
18	  `unop_epi_of_mono`. (2) `gf_flat_of_isEpi`: `[Algebra.IsEpi A R][Module.Flat A M]` (tower `A→R→M`) ⟹
19	  `Module.Flat R M`, 3 lines: `IsBaseChange.of_equiv (TensorProduct.lid' A R M)` (`lid'` from
20	  `Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Epi`) + `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`. Lesson: before accepting a "Mathlib-absent base
21	  change" verdict, search `Algebra.Epi`/`TensorProduct.lid'`/`CommRingCat.epi_iff_epi`.
22	- **Effective descent of a glued sheaf of modules = equalizer of pushforwards, NOT a hand-built
23	  compatible-families presheaf (iter-056, GR; closed the multi-iter `glue` keystone).** Build
24	  `Scheme.Modules.glue` as `Limits.equalizer a b` of two maps `∏ᶜ i, (D.ι i)_* (M i) ⇉ ∏ᶜ (i,j), (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* M i)`
25	  (`j_ij = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i`). Leg `a`: project to factor `i`, apply `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f_ij)` **unit**,
26	  push by `D.ι i`, reassoc via `pushforwardComp`. Leg `b`: factor `j`, adjunction unit for `t_ij ≫ f_ji`, push by
27	  `D.ι j`, `pushforwardComp`, transport across `(g i j).inv`, `pushforwardCongr` along the glue condition
28	  `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ι_j = f_ij ≫ ι_i`. `X.Modules` `HasLimits` (Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`), so
29	  the equalizer exists abstractly and is automatically a sheaf (pushforward + limits preserve the sheaf condition).
30	  The cocycle hyps `_hC1`/`_hC2` are NOT needed to construct the object (they pin downstream
31	  `glueRestrictionIso`/`glue_unique`). The hand-built compatible-families presheaf IS this equalizer — building it
32	  by hand re-derives the same object at far greater cost.
33	- **PresheafOfModules ℤ-tensor naturality is blocked by an `obj`-carrier vs `presheaf.obj`-carrier syntactic gap
34	  (iter-056, SNAP; `relTensorActL` blocker, NOT yet resolved).** The `tmul` induction element `m ⊗ₜ …` from
35	  `TensorProduct.induction_on` on `↥(P.obj U)` lives in the `P.obj`-carrier tensor; the only ℤ-linear restriction
36	  Mathlib provides (`(P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap`) has domain `↥((P.presheaf).obj U)`. These are rfl-defeq
37	  but syntactically distinct, so `TensorProduct.map_tmul`'s LHS won't unify. The reduction succeeds in isolation
38	  WHENEVER carriers agree. Changing the `obj` carriers to `(P.presheaf).obj ·` CASCADES (breaks proven
39	  `relTensorDomainPresheaf.map_id`/`map_comp`). Untried handle: build a DISTINCT `↥(P.obj ·)`-carrier ℤ-linear
40	  restriction (from `P.map f`/`ModuleCat.Hom.hom`) and use it uniformly so element & map carriers agree by
41	  construction. See [[snap-presheaf-promotion-carrier-gap]]. **RESOLVED iter-058** — the untried handle WORKED:
42	  `private def objRestrict P f` (a `↥(P.obj U)`-carrier ℤ-linear restriction) + `objRestrict_id`/`objRestrict_comp`
43	  laws. Follow-ons: (a) for *functoriality* avoid element-level `map_tmul` — collapse each ⊗-factor to
44	  `LinearMap.id`/comp at the LinearMap level (`objRestrict_id`/`_comp` + inline `hR` for the ring factor), then
45	  `TensorProduct.map_id`/`map_comp` + `rfl`; the diamond never bites at LinearMap level. (b) for *naturality*
46	  (actL/actR) close the tmul leaf by `change`-to-fully-reduced-form + `congr 1` + `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
47	  (the single math fact); `objRestrict_apply` bridges to the abelian restriction by defeq. GOTCHA: `P.presheaf`
48	  is `Ab`/`AddCommGrpCat`-valued ⇒ use `AddCommGrpCat.hom_id`/`hom_comp`, NOT `ModuleCat.*`. The carrier gap
49	  reappears on the MIDDLE ring factor of nested triple/projection tensors (`objRestrict` only fixes P/Q).
50	- **Flatness transfers across a ring-iso + semilinear additive-iso via base change — `flat_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`
51	  (iter-058, GF; the general workhorse for `eqToHom`/presheaf-iso flatness transport).** Given `e : R ≃+* R'`,
52	  `l : M ≃+ M'` with `l (r•x) = e r • l x`, and `Module.Flat R M`: `M'` is the base change `R'⊗[R]M` (lift of `l`
53	  is `R'`-linear, explicit inverse `y↦1⊗l⁻¹y`), so `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` descends flatness. Companion
54	  `flat_localization_models` makes flatness model-independent (two localizations `Rₛ,Mₛ` vs `Rₛ',Mₛ'` of the same
55	  `A`-module) via `IsLocalization.algEquiv`(`.toRingEquiv`) + `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` semilinearity. Pitfalls:
56	  (i) `open scoped TensorProduct in` goes on the line ABOVE the docstring (not between docstring and `theorem`,
57	  else "expected token"); (ii) route the ring-iso through `IsLocalization.algEquiv … .toRingEquiv` to dodge a
58	  `MulActionHomClass (Rₛ≃+*Rₛ') …` synth failure. To transport flatness along a presheaf-section equality
59	  `hbg : D g' = D g` whose target type DEPENDS on `D g` (`rw [←hbg]`/`hbg ▸` give an ill-typed motive), use
60	  `flat_of_ringEquiv_semilinear (RingEquiv.refl _) (F.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso …)).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv ?_`.
